content.problemStatement:
Maheshmati and Sangu are playing a game. First, Maheshmati gives Sangu a sequence of NNN distinct integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \dots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč (not necessarily sorted) and an integer KKK. Sangu has to create all subsequences of this sequence with length KKK. For each subsequence, he has to write down the product of K‚ąí2K-2K‚ąí2 integers: all elements of this subsequence except the minimum and maximum element.

Sangu wins the game if he is able to write down all these numbers and tell Maheshmati their product (modulo 109+710^9+7109+7, since it can be very large). However, Sangu is a very lazy child and thus wants you to help him win this game. Compute the number Sangu should tell Maheshmati!
constraints: the numbers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \dots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč are pairwise distinct
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \dots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the product of all numbers written down by Sangu modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There are four possible subsequences:

[1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] (Sangu should write down 222.)
[1,3,4][1, 3, 4][1,3,4] (Sangu should write down 333.)
[1,2,4][1, 2, 4][1,2,4] (Sangu should write down 222.)
[2,3,4][2, 3, 4][2,3,4] (Sangu should write down 333.)

The required product is 2‚čÖ3‚čÖ2‚čÖ3=362 \cdot 3 \cdot 2 \cdot 3 = 362‚čÖ3‚čÖ2‚čÖ3=36.
